note: The unfold direction is now the committed UI direction (the pre-unfold editor is frozen at sandbox/legacy, tag legacy-editor). The reading-mode beachhead landed as src/panel/unfold.ts — a full-screen folded view of ctx.state (planner isolation pattern), gate-verified in _bundle.mmd. This plan enumerates the REMAINING feature-wiring so nothing the editor does is lost as the unfold surface becomes primary. Every change is an ADD of its keystone symbol so flowmap:status derives real pending/built state from code — a change reads BUILT only when its symbol exists (symbol names are review-adjustable; keep them falsifiable). Resume any time: npm run flowmap:status -- --plan docs/flowmap/plans/unfold-integration.plan.json

phases.title: read is a first-class lens
phases.subtitle: the folded view shares the editor's session state

phases.title: acting from the reading surface
phases.subtitle: the first write capabilities, through the existing hooks

phases.title: review converges on the folded surface
phases.subtitle: diff/plan review and trust tiers inside reading mode

phases.title: one geometry, first-class grouping
phases.subtitle: shared wire geometry; hierarchy overlay becomes a directive

intent.problem: Selection dies at the mode boundary: a node selected in reading mode is forgotten when you return to the editor, and vice versa — the reader loses their place.
intent.approach: selectSync(): on open, seed SEL from the editor's current selection; on close, hand the reading selection back (selectOnly + zoomToNode via deps/hooks). No new state — the two surfaces share one selection.

intent.problem: A reading session (what is unfolded, which layers are on) evaporates on close/reload; re-orienting costs the reader every time.
intent.approach: persistView(): store expanded/hidden/layers per diagram alongside the existing persistence (core/persistence prefs pattern), restored on open. A NEVER-read diagram still arrives fully folded, all layers off.

intent.problem: Reading mode is read-only; the smallest correction (a rename) forces a full mode switch back to the dense editor.
intent.approach: renameInPlace(): inline rename on the selected card (Enter / double-click on selected), writing through the existing model path — mutate ctx.state, then ctx.hooks.sync + pushHistory + persist. Never a private write path.

intent.problem: desc/interfaces (the map's advisory + verified claims) can only be edited in the editor's inspector; the reading inspector shows them dead.
intent.approach: mountFrontmatter(): mount the existing frontmatter editor (panel/inspector-frontmatter) inside the reading inspector for the selected node, writing through the same hooks path as renameInPlace.

intent.problem: Plan/diff review (D2's unified surface) still opens the dense planner overlay; the human review that flowmap exists for does not benefit from the folded, opt-in reading surface.
intent.approach: planLayer(): render ctx.plan status (add/modify/remove tint + accept/reject) as a reading-mode LAYER over the folded cards — blast radius already lives there. planner keeps verdict/export logic; unfold supplies the surface. D-phase convergence — design-review before build.

intent.problem: The sandbox's trust layer (advisory descs, A5 advisory edges, tier chips) did not come along into the app, so the in-app reader cannot see claim strength.
intent.approach: trustLayer(): port the trust layer with an optional advisory source — same-origin edge-advisory-allowlist.txt when present (this repo), a Load button otherwise (any repo). Absent source = layer disabled, never wrong.

intent.problem: unfold draws its own naive orthogonal elbows; render/wires owns the app's real port-side geometry and avoidance. Two wire geometries will drift.
intent.approach: wirePath(): compute reading-mode wires from portPos/bestSides (core/state) and the wires elbow path — the one-way reuse the arch sandbox proved — optionally avoidRouter for dense unfolds. Replaces the local orthoPath.

intent.problem: The two-region responsibility grouping that makes arrival calm lives in sandbox/unfold/hierarchy.json — a curated overlay outside the gate; the app's reading mode cannot see it and other repos cannot author one.
intent.approach: parseGroupDirective(): promote the overlay to a first-class grouping directive in the .mmd dialect (e.g. %% group), parsed by io/mermaid (A3 conformance follows), emitted by the tooling, validated like %% parent; hierarchy.json is then deleted. Touches the gated dialect — design-review before build.
